summary: Data-subject rights: versioned consent banner, asynchronous "download my data" export, and hard account deletion across every enabled feature.
env: GDPR_EXPORT_ROOT — filesystem directory for export archives (default ./_exports); archives are envelope-encrypted at rest.
GDPR_EXPORT_TTL_HOURS — how long a completed export stays downloadable (default 168 = 7 days).
GDPR_POLICY_VERSION — the privacy-notice version in force (default 1); the app re-prompts when a user's recorded consent names a different one.
userSteps: Write your privacy notice and set GDPR_POLICY_VERSION; bump it whenever the notice changes so every user is asked again.
Export and erasure cover the features enabled in this workspace via IPersonalDataContributor — any feature you add yourself must register one to be included.
The export archives live under GDPR_EXPORT_ROOT on the API host; for multi-instance deployments back it with shared storage and replace the in-process export queue.
In production apply the schema and RLS with `dotnet ef database update`; in Development the app creates them at startup.
